headlines.title: Multiple hurt after police pursuit ends in San Francisco crash
headlines.description: Nine people were hurt, including two with life-threatening injuries, in a multi-car crash outside an Audi dealership in San Francisco on Sunday night. Police said the crash happened around 8 p.m. after officers spotted a car carrying five people driving erratically near Dolores Park. According to police, when officers tried to stop the vehicle, the driver sped away. The suspect’s car eventually crashed into another vehicle outside the Audi dealership at 14th Street and Van Ness Avenue. That vehicle then struck the dealership’s showroom windows. Police said the suspect’s car also hit a fire hydrant. In total, nine people were injured. Three were taken to the hospital, including two with life-threatening injuries. The driver of the suspect vehicle was detained along with the other four occupants. This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.
headlines.source: NBC Bay Area staff and Thom Jensen

headlines.title: Antioch police ask for help finding 2 boys
headlines.description: Antioch police are asking for the public’s help finding two boys who were last seen a week ago. Isaiah Figueroa, 12, and Gianni Figueroa, 13, were last seen on July 13 at about 6 p.m. near the intersection of Redwood and Gentrytown drives in Antioch, according to an endangered missing advisory put out Monday by the California Highway Patrol on behalf of the Antioch Police Department. Authorities are not referring to the case as an abduction, but the boys are listed as victims in the alert and 33-year-old Yesenia Villasenor is listed as a suspect. They might be traveling in a white 2019 Toyota Highlander with a license plate number of 8KJR109, according to the CHP. Isaiah is described as 5 feet 2 inches tall, weighing 120 pounds, with black hair and brown eyes. He was last seen wearing a black hoodie, black shorts and black slides. Gianni is described as 5 feet 7 inches tall, weighing 156 pounds, with black hair and brown eyes and wearing a white t-shirt. Villasenor is described as 5 feet 4 inches tall, weighing 137 pounds, with brown hair and brown eyes. Police didn’t say what kind of relationship, if any, she has with the boys. The alert was issued for Contra Costa and San Joaquin counties. Anyone with information should call 911. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: BART adds longer trains during peak commute hours
headlines.description: BART riders can expect more room during their commute starting Monday as the transit agency rolls out longer trains on several lines during peak travel times. Most trains on the Red, Yellow, Blue and Green lines will now operate with eight- or 10-car trains during peak commute hours. Shorter trains will continue to run during non-peak hours and on the Orange line. BART says the longer trains will provide more space, giving more commuters a chance to find a seat instead of standing. On the Red Line, 10-car trains will run during peak periods. The Yellow Line will add 10-car trains for its eight busiest morning commutes and six busiest commutes overall. The Green Line will add two more 8-car trains heading toward San Francisco in the morning, while the Blue Line will add one more 8-car train heading toward the East Bay in the afternoon. On the Orange Line, four trains will operate with five cars, while the remaining trains will have six cars. BART is also making schedule changes beginning Aug. 10. In addition to continuing the longer trains, the agency will adjust some departure times by a few minutes to, in BART’s words, “enhance reliability and reduce wait times.” Riders are encouraged to visit bart.gov to see how the changes may affect their commute.

headlines.title: Redwood City man accused of strangling brother surrenders after standoff
headlines.description: A Redwood City man was arrested after he barricaded himself inside a home for several hours following an alleged assault that left his younger brother unconscious over the weekend, police said. Officers responded to the 1000 block of Madison Avenue at 8:47 p.m. Saturday after receiving a report of a disturbance between family members. Officers said a 21-year-old man told them his brother, 37-year-old Orlando Escobedo Avila of Redwood City, had strangled him until he lost consciousness. The victim had injuries consistent with strangulation, the Redwood City Police Department said in a statement Monday. According to police, Escobedo Avila then barricaded himself inside the residence and repeatedly refused to surrender. Officers obtained an arrest warrant. After several hours of negotiations, Escobedo Avila surrendered and was taken into custody without injury. He was booked into the San Mateo County Maguire Correctional Facility on suspicion of felony assault and resisting or delaying officers. Police also served him with a previously unserved restraining order and a new emergency protective order issued on behalf of the victim. Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Police Department at 650-780-7100.

headlines.title: Woman accused of entering stranger's home in Santa Rosa and picking up child
headlines.description: A 23-year-old woman was accused of kidnapping by the Santa Rosa Police Department on Sunday after allegedly entering a stranger’s residence the day before and interacting with a child. Nyla Cristina Ponce was arrested after police said she entered the home of someone who did not know her in the 1500 block of Colgan Creek Drive at about 6:40 p.m. Saturday. She was accused of entering the strangers’ residence, picking up a young child, and carrying him upstairs, police said in a statement. The child was not reportedly injured and was out of his mother’s sight for about 30 seconds, police said. Ponce was accused of putting the child down upstairs before going into a bathroom and rummaging through drawers. She then allegedly fled the residence on foot. Santa Rosa police officers allegedly found her shortly afterward at Hearn Avenue and Dutton Meadow. Ponce, who is a Santa Rosa resident, was accused of striking a police officer with a water bottle during her arrest. The officer was not seriously injured, according to the police department. She was booked into Sonoma County Jail on multiple allegations, including kidnapping, burglary, and child endangerment. Police said the circumstances around the incident were still under investigation Sunday and the motive was unknown at that time.

headlines.title: Caltrans releases Bay Area transit plan to improve access on state highways
headlines.description: Caltrans has released a new Bay Area Transit Needs Assessment to help guide investments in transit-supportive infrastructure across the region’s state highway network. The assessment, released by Caltrans District 4, identifies locations where improvements such as bus-only lanes, safer crosswalks, and enhanced bicycle and pedestrian facilities could improve transit access and reliability. According to transport officials, it accompanies the Caltrans Bay Area Transit Plan, completed in 2025, which outlines the agency’s vision, goals, and strategies for supporting public transportation. According to Caltrans, the plan is the state’s first district-level transit plan is intended to serve as a model for regional transit planning in California. Together, the plan and assessment are designed to help expand transit access, increase ridership, and improve safety and mobility across the nine-county Bay Area. The assessment comes with an interactive online map that highlights locations where improvements could make transit easier and more accessible. The map can be found at caltrans.com.

headlines.title: Fans gather in San Francisco to watch Spain-Argentina World Cup final
headlines.description: Fans in San Francisco’s Thrive City cheered as they watched Spain come out on top over Argentina in the World Cup final on Sunday. The big screen at Thrive City had been a popular spot for World Cup watch parties, and Sunday’s was not the exception. With the match scoreless after 90 minutes and heading into extra time, fans watched every minute closely, reacting to it all. Sunday’s win represents the second men’s title for Spain, and fans in Thrive City who waited for this day were beaming with joy. “So happy Spain won, they are the better team. Argentina didn’t have any shots on goal, Spain dominated they deserve it. Second win, second World Cup win, we’re so happy and thrilled,” Dubai resident Ammar said. “I am feeling like a pure happiness I don’t even know how to explain I am so happy,” San Francisco resident Guiani said. Although not everyone in attendance was a fan of Argentina or Spain, they still wanted to be part of the energy during the final.

headlines.title: World Cup brought a boost to Bay Area businesses. Owners hope to keep the momentum going
headlines.description: Throughout the Bay Area, Spain celebrated the team’s World Cup win. Many gathered at Macarena, a Spanish restaurant in Palo Alto, to watch the team win the final match. The restaurant was full and the celebrations continued with Spanish tapas. People were hugging, jumping and then there was a spontaneous dance party and singing on the sidewalk. Reactions were just as explosive in San Pedro square in San Jose, where thousands gathered for the final watch party. But it’s not just Spain that was the big winner. Since June 11, the Bay Area has hosted dozens of viewing parties which have in some cases doubled or even tripled the number of customers at local restaurants and bars. Now some business owners hope that the new customers who visited them during World Cup events will return and keep the momentum going.

headlines.title: Apartment fire prompts traffic delays and evacuations in San Francisco
headlines.description: Authorities were urging the public to avoid a section of Jones Street due to an ongoing multi-floor apartment fire in San Francisco on Sunday. Three people were being evaluated with smoke inhalation, and the American Red Cross has been called, according to the San Francisco Fire Department. Authorities said emergency crews were in the area of Ellis Street and Steveloe Place. Just before 4 p.m., firefighters said the incident was resolved and emergency crews were clear of the scene. They asked the public to still expect residual traffic delays in the area and allow for additional travel time.

headlines.title: Sausalito city manager arrested while on leave
headlines.description: Sausalito City Manager Elaine Forbes was arrested Saturday morning while on a leave of absence, according to city officials. Authorities said Forbes was arrested at 6:50 a.m. and booked on criminal charges. Officials did not release any information regarding those charges. “This is a difficult development for our City staff and our community,” the city said in a statement. “Because this is now an active and ongoing investigation by the Marin County Sheriff’s Office, the City will not comment on the specific allegations.” Forbes took office as city manager on July 1, according to the city’s website. Her leave of absence was approved during a closed session city council meeting on Thursday. Forbes’ family released the following statement: “Elaine Forbes has devoted much of her life to public service and is widely respected by the many people who have worked with her over the years. We ask that no one rush to judgment based on limited information. Elaine is experiencing a mental health crisis. As the facts become clearer and Elaine obtains legal and healthcare counsel in the coming hours and days, we hope people will respond with compassion, patience and respect for her and her family during an extraordinarily difficult time.”

headlines.title: Pedestrian critical after being struck by a vehicle in San Jose
headlines.description: A pedestrian was taken to the hospital in critical condition after being hit by a vehicle, just after another vehicle crashed into their shopping cart on Saturday, police said. The police department said the crash happened just before 11 a.m. in the area of Leigh Avenue and Patio Drive. According to officers, a vehicle crashed with the pedestrian’s shopping cart; when the vehicle stopped, a second vehicle struck the pedestrian. Authorities said the pedestrian was taken to the hospital in critical condition, but medical staff advised the pedestrian is expected to survive through the night. They added the drivers remained at the scene.

headlines.title: SF restaurant receives backlash after using AI for menu pictures
headlines.description: A San Francisco restaurant is facing some backlash after the owners used AI to create photos of their menu items. They posted it on their window, not thinking much of it, but what followed were bad reviews and even vandalism. Lyndsey Lozano and her husband just opened the doors at Grind and Unwind a couple of months ago, with a focus on serving coffee and pastries made by Lozano herself – along with drinks at their bar. “It’s a touristy area, we thought let’s put some pictures up, let’s put signs up so they know what we have, it as just a temporary sign, to kind of let everyone know we are here… but it did not get the response we were expecting,” Lozano said. She created a sign with pictures of drink and food items and put it on their window, but soon after got a lot of reaction online. “A lot of people were like ‘If that’s what your food looks like, I don’t want to eat there.’ Adn we thought, we know it’s AI, we’re not pretending we’re something else. Other people were like ‘Is that really how much effort you put into your business?’,” she said. About a week ago, she says someone spray painted what looks to say “seriously” outside. They’ve now painted over it and she’s taken down the menu she created using AI. Now she’s looking to move forward and is now put her pastries by the window for people to see as they walk by. “We’re focusing on our good pastries, on our coffee, we have a really good burger,” Lozano said. “We’re trying to make a really nice spot for locals to hang out, to be with each other and enjoy really delicious baked goods.”
